features.title: Global card network
features.description: Provides payment network rails used by issuers and acquirers to route and authorize Visa card transactions worldwide.

features.title: Multiple card types
features.description: Supports credit, debit, and prepaid Visa-branded cards issued by participating financial institutions.

features.title: In-person and online payments
features.description: Used for card-present transactions (POS) and card-not-present e-commerce and in-app payments via acquiring/payment gateway integrations.

features.title: Security and standards
features.description: Operates under industry standards such as EMV and PCI DSS in collaboration with the payments ecosystem.

features.title: Dispute and chargeback process
features.description: Includes rules and processes for chargebacks and dispute management between issuers, acquirers, and merchants.

features.title: Tokenization support
features.description: Supports network tokenization in partnership with issuers and wallet providers to enhance security in digital payments.

seo_title: Visa payments integration: cards, network rails, and issuing
seo_description: Visa is a global payments technology company and card network. Learn about Visa payment acceptance, card present and online transactions, and common integration
keywords: Visa, card network, credit card, debit card, payments, acquiring, issuing, VisaNet
excerpt: Visa is a global payments technology company and card network used for credit, debit, and prepaid card transactions.

availability.note: Visa is available globally, subject to local issuing, acquiring, and regulatory requirements.

tip: Most merchants integrate Visa via their payment gateway or acquirer rather than directly with Visa; check with your acquirer for Visa acceptance enablement and requirements.

faq.question: Can I integrate directly with Visa to accept payments?
faq.answer: Typically, merchants accept Visa through a payment gateway and an acquiring bank (acquirer). Direct integration with Visa is less common and usually applies to specific Visa programs or partners.

faq.question: Does Visa issue cards to consumers?
faq.answer: No. Visa-branded cards are generally issued by banks and other licensed financial institutions; Visa operates the network and sets rules and standards.

faq.question: Is Visa available worldwide?
faq.answer: Visa operates globally, though availability can depend on local issuing/acquiring coverage and regulatory restrictions.

faq.question: Where can I find developer documentation?
faq.answer: Visa offers developer resources and APIs through Visa Developer (developer.visa.com), but most merchant payment integrations are handled through a gateway or acquirer.
